because.text: The constitution promoted through its own process, not by fiat. A second 3-family gate on the promotion question (Gemini 3.1 Pro, GPT-5, DeepSeek v4 Pro): unanimous PROMOTE-WITH-AMENDMENTS. Adjudicated: (P0, Gemini alone 1/3) the scoped key-state refusal deadlocks the append-only settlement closure permanently after the FIRST roster rotation - the very operation §4 exists to support - and the static vector GV-KEYSTATE-QUORUM-REFUSED had enshrined it; two static-vector-minded reviewers praised the mechanism that self-destructs. Fixed with an OPTIONAL resolved_key_state acknowledgment in sigma-glyph.anchor-trust@v1 (rotation derived into actors out-of-band no longer refuses; unacknowledged still does), plus a transition vector GV-KEYSTATE-RESOLVED, in BOTH implementations (Python+Go, differential 24/24) - the fix is warranted regardless of promotion since the bug shipped in v0.6.3. (P1, 3/3) §0 defines STANDARD (frozen schemas @v2-for-breaking, frozen §3 mechanism, backward-compat/fail-closed, SemVer); normative deps pinned - Warrant v0.3 by snapshot SHA-256 73758bdb, Books I-III by anchored version - so a STANDARD never rests on a moving DRAFT target. (P2, GPT-5) in-tree-trust MUST in §3 step 1, release-advisory, closure/signature note. Coherence resolved 3/3: constitution stabilizing before its governed content is a feature. Document version 1.0.0 (3/3), independent of the v0.6.4 bundle. This gate earned its keep: without Gemini's P0 we would have labeled STANDARD a mechanism that dies on first use.

subject.note: GOV STANDARD promotion gate: DRAFT STANDARD -> STANDARD (v1.0.0), 3-family unanimous PROMOTE-WITH-AMENDMENTS
